title: Verify ITCH license terms for download-at-user-time
description: Confirm NASDAQ's redistribution rules permit the download.sh approach: users fetch raw ITCH at runtime; no ITCH bytes are committed to the repo. CRITICAL: QBBO/Nasdaq-Basic published BBO file is SEPARATELY LICENSED — must NOT be referenced as a verification target (Codex review caught this). License finding goes into REPRODUCIBILITY.md.
close_reason: Documented download-at-user-time stance, raw ITCH no-vendor rule, and QBBO/Nasdaq Basic exclusion in REPRODUCIBILITY.md

title: Plot: book-reconstruction snapshots at N timestamps
description: Pick N=3 random timestamps within the replay window; render the full LOB state (bid/ask ladder) at each. Visual sanity check that the engine reconstructs realistic book shapes from raw ITCH.
close_reason: Replay JSONL now carries top-five bid/ask ladders, and report.py renders three deterministic book reconstruction snapshot tables.

title: OCaml unit tests on book.ml + matching.ml + replay.ml
description: Independent of Rust↔OCaml differential testing. Standalone Alcotest (or stdlib-only) unit tests covering: book.ml insert/cancel/find at edge prices, matching.ml each FOK/IOC/STP variant, replay.ml malformed-input handling. Target: ≥90% line coverage of oracle-ocaml/lib/. Required by canonical 'comprehensive unit tests' rule. NOT a substitute for the differential corpus — both layers needed.

title: cargo-public-api baseline lock
description: Run cargo public-api (or cargo-semver-checks) to capture current public surface. Commit baseline. CI fails on unexpected API changes — note: baseline is DOCUMENTATION, not a contract (pre-1.0 changes are allowed but visible).

title: Golden corpus of LOB edge cases
description: REPLACES the original 'must find >=1 divergence' criterion (theater per Codex). Hand-curated event sequences: cancel-at-zero-qty, multiple fills at same price, FOK no-match, FOK partial-cross, each StpPolicy variant, min/max price, simultaneous timestamps. Both engines produce byte-identical output on the corpus.

title: Deprecation cleanup (items deprecated since v0.9 or earlier)
description: Remove any #[deprecated] items that have been on notice since v0.9. Pre-1.0 = breaking allowed. List removals in CHANGELOG.

title: Scaffold oracle-ocaml/ as sibling dune project
description: NOT a Cargo workspace member. Layout: oracle-ocaml/{dune-project, lib/, bin/, test/}. CRITICAL invariants: cargo add nanobook does NOT pull OCaml; pip install nanobook wheel build does NOT require OCaml; default cargo test runs without OCaml installed. Verified by sanity-check CI jobs.

title: Write docs/staying-0x.md (CENTRAL argument)
description: THE central artifact. Explicit argument why nanobook stays pre-1.0: solo-maintained = cannot underwrite 1.0 stability indefinitely; 0.x lets us evolve LOB schema, audit format, broker trait as we learn; fork-at-tag is the right path for users needing 1.0-style stability. Includes 'Conditions to revisit 1.0' section: ≥3 active maintainers, ≥6 months stable schema, demonstrated user base.

title: Design v0.12 report mockup before strategy implementation
description: Generate mockups/v0.12-momentum-backtest.html with synthetic data using uPlot. Mockup defines visual spec for: equity curve (nanobook vs vectorbt parity), drawdown chart, turnover by month, cost decomposition stacked bars. Visual lockup happens BEFORE strategy.py emits its first chart so we iterate in plan space, not in code.

title: CI examples-smoke job for 1-min slice
description: GitHub Actions job that runs on every PR: downloads the deterministic 1-minute slice (via download.sh against committed sample.md5), runs the cargo example, runs report.py, diffs the summary output against expected/. Full-day replay stays OUT of CI (too big). Cache the slice.
close_reason: Added GitHub Actions itch-replay-smoke job: downloads 1-min ITCH slice via download.sh, runs cargo example, runs report.py, diffs summary.txt against expected/, verifies invariants.log empty, caches ITCH data

title: Implement download_prices.py (S&P 100 OHLCV)
description: Python script fetching daily OHLCV via stooq or yfinance for the chosen universe. Caches results with checksum. Same download-at-runtime pattern as v0.11. Survivorship bias disclosed in README.

title: Crowbar property-test harness on OCaml side
description: AFL-style fuzzer that generates random valid event streams, replays through both engines, asserts trade-output equality. Deterministic seeds in CI; record failing seeds for replay.

title: Scaffold examples/paper-live-ibkr/ (runner.sh + risk-config.toml)
description: Cron-friendly runner.sh using rebalancer --cron-mode (from v0.13 F7). Published risk-config.toml documenting the exact limits used during the soak. Universe choice TBD: S&P 100 monthly (v0.12 default) or 10 names weekly for clearer audit-log storytelling.

title: Emit LOB invariant self-check log
description: Replaces the (dropped) BBO-against-published-file check. Four invariants checked at every event: (1) no crossed book (best_bid < best_ask always), (2) timestamps monotonic per-symbol, (3) every cancel reduces resting qty for that order (or removes it), (4) aggregate volume conserved across event boundaries. Any violation logged with event context; log file empty == pass.
close_reason: Replay writes invariants.log and checks crossed books, per-symbol timestamp monotonicity, known cancel/execute reductions, and aggregate resting-volume bounds; smoke slice log is empty.

title: Soak execution: 2-4 calendar weeks of live paper trading
description: MIN 14 trading days; target 2-4 weeks. Daily check-ins (audit-log review, equity update); weekly summaries written down. RUNS IN PARALLEL with v0.15 OCaml work on a separate branch. Any uncovered failure -> stop, patch v0.13, restart soak from day 0.

title: Write examples/momentum-backtest/COMPARISON.md
description: Line-by-line: nanobook vs vectorbt. Where they agree (zero cost), where they don't (with costs), why. This is the educational artifact.

title: v0.13 release: CI extension + CHANGELOG + version bumps + tag
description: Failure-injection suite (including F-bin1 + F-bin2 Binance modes) runs on every PR. CHANGELOG [0.13.0] entry. Version bumps (per-crate semver): nanobook 0.12.0->0.13.0; nanobook-broker 0.5.0->0.6.0 (BREAKING); nanobook-risk 0.5.0 untouched; nanobook-rebalancer 0.6.0->0.7.0 (BREAKING: new --cron-mode + --kill flags); nanobook-python 0.12.0->0.13.0. Tag + push + publish in dependency order.

title: OCaml toolchain setup (opam, dune, deps)
description: opam switch with stable OCaml (5.x), dune 3.x, jbuilder. Add Crowbar for v0.16 property tests. NO Base/Core (Codex recommendation: stdlib is lighter, ~800 LOC target). CI install + cache via opam-installer action.

title: Plot: top-of-book spread distribution
description: Histogram of (best_ask - best_bid) in cents across the full replay window. Sanity check that spreads cluster at 1c for liquid symbols, wider for thin.
close_reason: Replay events now include best_bid, best_ask, and spread; report.py renders a spread-in-cents SVG histogram from the JSONL stream.

title: v0.13 F-bin2: Binance reconnect drill
description: Mirror v0.13 F6 (TWS restart) for Binance: WebSocket drop + reconnect during open positions. Reconcile state from Binance account-info endpoint, no double-submits. Same 30s reconcile target as IBKR.

title: Public-surface audit per crate -> docs/api-surface-audit.md
description: Every pub item across all crates inventoried: stability status, deprecation candidates, doc completeness. Same audit you'd do for a 1.0 candidate, different framing — informs the 'staying-0x' argument by showing exactly what would be promised under 1.0.

title: Formalize docs/event-log-schema.md with schema_version field
description: The contract between Rust LOB (emits) and OCaml oracle (consumes). Explicit schema_version field in every event. Required for v0.16's schema-versioning policy. Both engines parse the same JSON schema doc — drift = CI failure.

title: v0.13 F9: process crash mid-rebalance + warm restart
description: SIGKILL the runner mid-rebalance. Restart reads audit log, determines exact point of failure, completes or rolls back cleanly. Test: kill at every audit-log checkpoint, assert correct final state in all cases.

title: Choose v0.12 strategy spec (momentum vs SMA)
description: Decide: cross-sectional momentum on S&P 100 (12mo lookback, top-decile L / bottom-decile S, equal weight, monthly rebal) vs simpler SMA crossover. Default: momentum — uncontroversial, peer-reviewed reference (Jegadeesh & Titman 1993 or Asness), readers can verify. Document choice in examples/momentum-backtest/README.md.

title: Update CHANGELOG.md with v0.11 entries
description: Keep a Changelog format. Section [0.11.0] — Date — 'Replay'. Subsections: Added (examples/itch-replay/, REPRODUCIBILITY.md), Changed (README perf section), Fixed (any ITCH parser gaps surfaced).

title: Implement examples/itch-replay/download.sh with md5 checksum
description: Bash script that curl-fetches the chosen ITCH .NASDAQ_ITCH50.gz file from emi.nasdaq.com, verifies md5sum against a committed expected/sample.md5, and refuses to proceed on mismatch. Idempotent: skips download if file already present and valid.
close_reason: Added idempotent ITCH download script, committed MD5, ignored local data directory, and verified existing download checksum

title: vectorbt parity check at zero transaction cost
description: Implement same strategy in vectorbt with zero costs. Compare equity curve, Sharpe, max DD. Goal: within epsilon. Differences ARE bugs at zero cost.

title: Mid-roadmap kill-gate decision
description: BINARY DECISION: continue to v0.15, or cut roadmap to 'v0.14 is the last release before a pause.' Pre-agreed criteria (decided BEFORE v0.13 started, not now): hours/week on nanobook over prior 8 weeks (kill if <5); unplanned v0.13 ops fixes (kill threshold >=3); v0.15 OCaml setup blockers in week 1 (kill if >5 working days lost); burnout self-report (hard stop). Decision committed to writing in docs/solutions/v0.14-kill-gate.md.

title: Plot: message-rate timeline
description: Time series of events/second across the trading day. Shows open/close burst patterns and any anomalous gaps that might indicate parser misses.
close_reason: report.py now buckets event timestamps per second and renders a message-rate SVG timeline in the generated HTML report.

title: Scaffold broker/tests/failure_injection/ harness
description: IBKR wire-level mock that injects each of the 9 failure modes deterministically. Pure Rust integration test (default) or Python-driven scenario runner — choose at planning gate. Mock surface mirrors TWS API: callback ordering, sequence numbers, partial-fill semantics.

title: scripts/sanitize-audit.py (publishable PII scrubber)
description: Removes account IDs, IBKR client IDs, order IDs while preserving audit-log invariants (sequence numbers, timestamps, order math, fill sequence). Idempotent — safe to re-run. Also supports --check mode for CI verification of committed audit excerpts.

title: v0.12 — Backtest + Positioning
description: Cross-sectional momentum case study with vectorbt parity check + competitive positioning table in README (flips defensive 'what nanobook is NOT' into offensive niche claim). 2-3 weeks. Depends on v0.11. See plan_v0.12.md (PREVIEW).

title: Emit summary stats text
description: Plain-text summary: total event count by type, total trades, unique symbols seen, total volume, time range. Used as the deterministic expected/ golden output for the 1-min CI slice.
close_reason: Replay now writes deterministic summary.txt with message counts, emitted event counts by type, total trades, unique symbols, total volume, and timestamp range.

title: vectorbt parity check with cost models on
description: Re-run both with realistic costs (e.g., 5 bps). Differences EXPECTED here — vectorbt and nanobook have different cost models. Document deviations educationally; not bugs.

title: Implement strategy.py using nanobook PyO3 portfolio API
description: Compute monthly signal, generate target weights, feed through nanobook::portfolio simulator via PyO3 bindings. Emits per-month rebalance events + equity curve.

title: .github/workflows/oracle.yml CI job
description: Install OCaml (cached opam), build oracle, run golden corpus, replay v0.11 ITCH 1-min slice through both engines, diff trades. Fails build on any divergence. Adds ~2-4 min per PR — cache aggressively. Also runs the sanity-check 'no OCaml in wheel' job from V15C.

title: Write docs/solutions/oracle-property-tests.md
description: What property tests caught vs what hand-written tests caught. Surprises from Crowbar. Practical guidance for adding new property generators when LOB semantics extend.

title: Pick NASDAQ TotalView-ITCH 5.0 source date
description: Choose a specific date from NASDAQ's public ITCH archive (emi.nasdaq.com, confirmed live during Codex review). Criteria: deep enough volume to exercise cancel-heavy flow (target: 2019-01-30 or later). Trade off file size (3.5-6 GB) vs scientific interest. Document the choice in REPRODUCIBILITY.md.
close_reason: Selected 2019-07-30 NASDAQ TotalView-ITCH source and documented URL, size, and checksum caveat in REPRODUCIBILITY.md

title: Emit JSONL event log from replay
description: One JSON object per line: timestamp, event type (add/cancel/execute/replace), price, qty, side, order_id. This is the contract the v0.15 OCaml oracle will later consume. Schema is provisional in v0.11 and formalized with schema_version field in v0.15. Format must be append-only and stream-parseable.
close_reason: Replay event-log.jsonl is stream-parseable and includes timestamp, type, price, qty, side, and canonical order_id fields where applicable.

title: Write docs/solutions/ops-hardening-learnings.md
description: Each of the 9 failure modes: was it already handled by v0.10 hardening, or did it surface a new bug? What did we change? This is the second entry in docs/solutions/.

title: Unstage accidental python/ deletion
description: Run 'git restore --staged python/' to undo the accidental staging of the entire python/ tree as deleted. Files themselves are not gone (D status, not ??). Must complete before any v0.11 work touches python/ — otherwise a later git add could re-stage the deletion. Verification: 'git status' shows python/ files unmodified.
close_reason: Verified python/ has no staged deletion

title: Document v0.14 kill-gate criteria BEFORE v0.13 starts
description: Write docs/solutions/v0.14-kill-gate-criteria.md PRE-COMMITTING the exact numerical thresholds for the kill-gate decision: (1) hours/week on nanobook over prior 8 weeks — kill if <5; (2) unplanned v0.13 ops fixes — kill threshold ≥3; (3) v0.15 OCaml setup blockers in week 1 — kill if >5 working days lost; (4) burnout self-report — hard stop. CRITICAL: this must be committed BEFORE any v0.13 sub-bead starts. The criteria become unfalsifiable if written after seeing the data.

title: Design v0.14 report mockup before paper-soak runner exists
description: Generate mockups/v0.14-paper-soak.html with synthetic data using uPlot. Mockup defines visual spec for: daily equity curve, position evolution per ticker, realized-vs-expected slippage scatter, incidents log table. The incidents log especially needs visual design — text-heavy operational events rendered alongside numeric plots. Lock the mockup BEFORE the soak begins so we know what we're capturing.

title: v0.13 F-bin1: Binance idempotency proof
description: Mirror v0.13 F7 (cron double-fire idempotency) for Binance broker adapter. Sequence-number/audit-log check that double-fire produces exactly one set of orders against the (mocked) Binance API. Lighter scope than IBKR full 9 modes — Codex review consensus was 'at least 2' for Binance.

title: v0.14 pre-soak rehearsal (1 week live IBKR paper)
description: 1-week dry-run on real IBKR paper account validating that v0.13's failure-injection hardening holds in the actual paper environment (not just synthetic mock). Any surprise = a v0.13.1 patch, not a v0.14 hack.

title: Write docs/ops/warm-restart.md
description: Audit-log → state reconstruction protocol with worked examples for operators. Documents what an operator should do after a crash: how to read the audit log, how to confirm position state matches IBKR's view, when to manually intervene.

title: Implement examples/itch-replay/replay.rs cargo example
description: Cargo example (or src/bin/) that streams ITCH 5.0 events from the downloaded sample through nanobook's LOB. Uses nanobook::itch parser. Outputs three artifacts: (a) JSONL event log, (b) summary stats text, (c) LOB invariant self-check log. Must run on a 16 GB laptop in <10 min wall time for the full chosen day.
close_reason: Added itch-replay cargo example producing JSONL event log, summary stats, and invariant log; verified example tests and workspace validators

title: v0.12 release: learnings doc + CHANGELOG + version bumps + tag
description: Write docs/solutions/portfolio-sim-parity-learnings.md. CHANGELOG [0.12.0] entry. Version bumps (per-crate semver): nanobook 0.11.0->0.12.0; nanobook-python 0.11.0->0.12.0; nanobook-broker 0.5.0 untouched; nanobook-risk 0.5.0 untouched; nanobook-rebalancer 0.6.0 untouched. Pre-tag verification suite. Tag + push + publish.

title: v0.17 — Why nanobook stays 0.x
description: INVERTED from original 1.0-flag plan. Explicitly argues why nanobook does NOT tag 1.0: solo-maintained project tagging 1.0 commits to maintenance it cannot underwrite; stale 1.0 worse than active 0.x. Ships docs/staying-0x.md + public-surface audit + SEMVER.md + MSRV pin. States conditions under which 1.0 could be revisited later. 1-2 weeks. See plan_v0.17.md (PREVIEW).

title: Write SEMVER.md (pre-1.0 policy)
description: Explicit statement: 0.x means minor versions MAY break. Covers: what counts as public API, what counts as breaking, what is explicitly NOT covered (event-log byte format, internal modules, #[doc(hidden)] items). Compare against a hypothetical 1.0 SEMVER (which would commit to major-only breaking).

title: README: measured perf numbers top-of-fold
description: Replace the current top-of-README perf claim with the measured p50/p95/p99 numbers from E3E, with explicit 'Measured on NASDAQ TotalView-ITCH <date>, hardware <model>, N=<event count>'. Reproducible by following REPRODUCIBILITY.md.
close_reason: Replaced top-of-README synthetic perf claims with measured p50/p95/p99 numbers from ITCH replay: parse (875ns/1000ns/3000ns) and book-update (1208ns/3667ns/6792ns) on NASDAQ TotalView-ITCH 2019-07-30, Apple M1 Pro, 16GB RAM, N=47,739 events; moved synthetic microbenchmarks to subsection; added reproducibility note

title: report.py skeleton + PyO3 event-log loader
description: Python script (NOT a notebook — diffable, no rot) that loads the JSONL event log via nanobook's PyO3 bindings. Pinned deps in examples/itch-replay/requirements.txt; runs via uv. Skeleton produces empty plots; subsequent beads fill in each visualization.
close_reason: Added examples/itch-replay/report.py plus requirements.txt; the script imports nanobook's PyO3 package, stream-loads event-log.jsonl, summarizes events, and emits an HTML skeleton with placeholder plots.

title: Finalize REPRODUCIBILITY.md with full metadata
description: Fill in every section started in E1F with actual values from the run that produced the published numbers: exact md5, hardware model (e.g., 'M1 Pro, 16 GB'), OS version, rustc version, Python version, uv.lock hash, CI slice params. Verified by running on a different machine.

title: README: methodology footnote linking to repro doc
description: Footnote next to both perf numbers pointing to examples/itch-replay/README.md and REPRODUCIBILITY.md. Explains the difference: measured = end-to-end on real data, synthetic = kernel-only microbench.

title: Cross-version replay gating in CI
description: Every PR replays v0.11 ITCH 1-min slice + v0.12 backtest event log + v0.14 paper-soak audit excerpt through both engines. Oracle becomes regression gate for ALL prior demos. Parallelize across CI runners.

title: oracle-ocaml/bin/triage.ml (bisect divergent inputs)
description: Given a divergent event stream, bisects to find the minimal failing input (target: ≤20 events). Speeds up debug when a new divergence appears. Stays CI-only — not packaged as user-facing tool (per invisibility constraint).

title: v0.13 F3: partial fill followed by disconnect
description: Order partially fills, then TWS drops. On reconnect, broker queries IBKR's open-positions for ground truth, observes partial state, does NOT double-submit the remainder. Test: inject partial-then-disconnect, assert correct reconciled state.

title: lib/matching.ml (exhaustive pattern matching)
description: Match on every event variant exhaustively. When the Rust LOB adds a new event type, OCaml compile fails until handled — that's the point. STP variants (each StpPolicy), FOK no-match, partial-cross all explicitly cased.

title: docs/solutions/oracle-design.md + dual-purpose declaration
description: Names the dual purpose honestly: (a) technical bug-finding for wrong-but-consistent class (Knight Capital) that fuzzing/mutants miss; (b) Jane Street audience signaling. Both are real; stating both prevents rationalization trap. Triage protocol for when divergence is found: which engine is wrong? Process documented up front.

title: 1k-seed initial run + divergence triage
description: Run Crowbar for 1k seeds. Each divergence: identify which engine is wrong, fix, add as a golden corpus case in v0.15-H so it never regresses.

title: Draft REPRODUCIBILITY.md skeleton
description: Initial structure of REPRODUCIBILITY.md at repo root. Sections: dataset URL, md5sum, hardware model + clock speed, OS version, Rust toolchain version, Python version, uv.lock hash, CI slice parameters, why raw licensed data is not vendored. Skeleton now, full metadata filled in E5A after a successful end-to-end run.
close_reason: Expanded REPRODUCIBILITY.md with dataset, checksum, licensing stance, CI slice parameters, commands, and environment metadata

title: Write docs/solutions/itch-replay-learnings.md
description: First entry in docs/solutions/ for nanobook. Captures: what surprised us during the replay, any residual ITCH parser gaps fixed in v0.11, candidate v0.12 follow-ups. Indexed in the docs/solutions/ tree.
close_reason: done

title: README: synthetic table moves to Kernel microbenchmarks subsection
description: Keep BOTH numbers (Codex+adversary agreed). The existing criterion microbench table (120-200 ns/order, 6M ops/sec) moves to a 'Kernel microbenchmarks' subsection with its existing methodology footnote — explicitly labeled as synthetic. Adding data > replacing data.
close_reason: done

title: lib/price.ml + lib/order.ml + lib/side.ml (types)
description: Integer Price.t newtype (cents, matches Rust). Side = Buy | Sell. Order variant covering Limit/Market/IOC/FOK with explicit fields. Written from spec (event-log schema) only, NOT by reading Rust source — that's what makes the oracle independent.

title: v0.13 F1: duplicate order-status callbacks
description: TWS occasionally re-sends order-status callbacks. Broker adapter must dedupe by (order_id, sequence) and not double-act. Test: inject 2 identical fills, assert position changes once.

title: v0.13 F5: clock skew between strategy host and exchange
description: NTP drift or VM clock jump. Must not silently corrupt audit-log timestamps or rebalance windowing. Test: skew clock by ±30s, assert detection + warning + audit-log integrity.

title: README 'Battle-tested' section + v0.14 release
description: Headline summary in README: calendar duration, number of rebalances, number of orders, number of reconciles, number of incidents. CHANGELOG [0.14.0]. Version bumps (per-crate semver): nanobook 0.13.0→0.14.0; nanobook-broker 0.6.0→0.6.1 (patch); nanobook-risk 0.5.0 untouched; nanobook-rebalancer 0.7.0→0.7.1 (patch); nanobook-python 0.13.0→0.14.0. NOTE: if soak surfaces a BREAKING bug, response is v0.13.1 patch + soak restart from day 0, not a v0.14 minor that rolls in breaking changes. Tag + push.

title: README competitive positioning table
description: Flip the defensive 'What nanobook is NOT' section into an offensive table: nanobook vs vectorbt vs NautilusTrader vs Hummingbot vs Freqtrade vs LEAN. Columns: scope (kernel/platform), execution model, Python API, Rust core, auditability, niche. Honest about weaknesses (no GUI, no community size) as well as strengths.

title: v0.16 release: CI seed cap + version bumps + tag
description: Cap Crowbar seed budget at 10k/PR for CI time. Parallelize replay across runners. CHANGELOG [0.16.0]. Version bumps (per-crate semver): nanobook 0.15.0→0.16.0; nanobook-broker 0.6.1 untouched; nanobook-risk 0.5.0 untouched; nanobook-rebalancer 0.7.1 untouched; nanobook-python 0.14.0 untouched. Tag + push.

title: v0.11 pre-tag checks + dry-run + tag + push
description: Full workspace verification before tag: cargo test --workspace --all-features; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --check; cargo deny check; maturin develop --release && uv run pytest tests/ -q; cargo publish --dry-run on each affected crate. Then tag v0.11.0, push, publish in dependency order.

title: v0.13 F4: stale market data detection
description: Snapshot held while quotes move (data feed lagged). Rebalancer must detect (last_quote_age > threshold) and abort the rebalance rather than trade on stale prices.

title: v0.13 F2: cancel reject (race with fill)
description: Cancel request races against an in-flight fill. IBKR rejects the cancel. Broker must reconcile: order is filled, cancel is moot, audit log captures both attempts. Test: inject fill-then-cancel-reject sequence.

title: v0.14 — Paper-Live Soak
description: 2-4 week IBKR paper-trade soak using v0.13's hardened plumbing. Runs in PARALLEL with v0.15 branch development so calendar time is off the critical path. Mid-roadmap KILL GATE after this release: pre-agreed cut criteria for v0.15-v0.17 (hours/week, ops-fix count, OCaml blocker time, burnout). See plan_v0.14.md (PREVIEW).

title: report.py -> report.html for soak
description: Daily equity curve, position evolution, every reconcile mismatch (if any), realized vs expected slippage, full list of operational incidents during the soak. Reconstructable from sanitized audit excerpts alone.

title: lib/book.ml (sorted association lists)
description: Deliberately slow and obvious: sorted assoc lists for price levels, not FxHashMap or skip list. Performance irrelevant. Correctness is the only goal. Insert/remove/find are O(n) — fine for oracle workloads.

title: Design v0.11 report mockup before report.py implementation
description: Generate mockups/v0.11-itch-replay.html with synthetic data using uPlot (chosen viz library). Mockup defines the visual spec for the four plots: latency distribution (3 stages overlapping), spread histogram, message-rate timeline, book-reconstruction snapshot table. Author opens in browser, iterates on style, then locks it. Implementation in E3A-F must match the mockup style. Without this, we don't know what the report looks like until code lands — too late to iterate.
close_reason: mockups/v0.11-itch-replay.html defines the uPlot visual lockup for latency, message-rate timeline, spread distribution, and book snapshot table using synthetic data.

title: Pin MSRV + CI matrix row
description: Pick minimum supported Rust version based on what demos actually need. Document in SEMVER.md and Cargo.toml. CI matrix gets an MSRV row that must pass.

title: v0.13 F8: kill switch (--kill subcommand)
description: New subcommand: rebalancer --kill. Sends SIGTERM to running runner via lockfile/PID, then verifies (via audit log query) that no working orders remain on the exchange. Test: start runner mid-rebalance, --kill, assert no dangling orders.

title: v0.13 — Ops Hardening
description: Production-readiness for broker+rebalancer path. 9 failure modes covered: duplicate callbacks, cancel reject, partial-fill+disconnect, stale data, clock skew, TWS restart, cron double-fire, kill switch, mid-rebalance crash. Splits 'paper-live' into hardening (v0.13) and soak (v0.14). 2-3 weeks. See plan_v0.13.md (PREVIEW).

title: v0.15 release: CHANGELOG + version bump + tag
description: CHANGELOG [0.15.0] entry. Version bumps (per-crate semver): nanobook 0.14.0->0.15.0 (oracle-found divergences fixed, event-log schema doc added with schema_version field); nanobook-broker 0.6.1 untouched; nanobook-risk 0.5.0 untouched; nanobook-rebalancer 0.7.1 untouched; nanobook-python 0.14.0 untouched (oracle is CI-only, zero Python-surface change). Pre-tag verification: cargo test workspace, clippy, fmt, oracle CI job green on golden corpus + v0.11 ITCH slice. cargo publish --dry-run. Tag v0.15.0 + push + publish only nanobook (other crates unchanged). NOTE: OCaml oracle code itself is NOT published to crates.io — repo-internal tooling only.

title: v0.13 F7: cron double-fire (--cron-mode idempotency)
description: New flag: rebalancer --cron-mode. Writes a sequence number per rebalance window to audit log; refuses to run if window already complete. Test: 1000 back-to-back invocations produce exactly one set of orders.

title: HTML report rendering + requirements.txt pin
description: report.py emits report.html with all four plots embedded. requirements.txt pins exact Python + matplotlib + pandas versions for reproducibility. uv-managed.
close_reason: Implemented latency distribution histogram with log-scale visualization showing parse vs book latencies (p50/p95/p99), pinned Python 3.14.0 and maturin 1.11.5 in requirements.txt for reproducibility, all four plots now render in report.html

title: v0.15 — OCaml Oracle (CI-only)
description: Independent OCaml LOB used CI-only for differential testing. Stock OCaml + Stdlib + Crowbar, ~800 LOC. Catches wrong-but-consistent bug class (Knight Capital). Dual purpose declared honestly: technical bug-finding + Jane Street audience signaling. Invisible to users — cargo/pip users see zero OCaml. 2-3 weeks, parallel branch during v0.14 soak. See plan_v0.15.md (PREVIEW).

title: v0.17 release: CHANGELOG + version bumps + tag
description: Per-crate semver release (NOT a unified workspace bump). CHANGELOG [0.17.0]. Version bumps: nanobook 0.16.0→0.17.0; nanobook-broker 0.6.1→0.7.0 (audit-driven breaking); nanobook-risk 0.5.0→0.6.0 (audit-driven breaking — first substantive change since v0.10); nanobook-rebalancer 0.7.1→0.8.0 (audit-driven breaking); nanobook-python 0.14.0→0.15.0 (follows upstream breaks). Synchronizing all crates to a single 0.17.0 would be marketing, not semver — risk has had effectively one change in seven releases. NO 1.0 tag planned. Tag + push + announce per docs/staying-0x.md.

title: v0.16 — Oracle Hardening
description: Take oracle from exists to load-bearing. Crowbar property tests (10k random streams/PR), cross-version replay gating (v0.11+v0.12+v0.14 event logs replayed through both engines on every PR), triage tool that bisects divergent inputs to ≤20 events, schema versioning policy. Still invisible to users. 1-2 weeks. See plan_v0.16.md (PREVIEW).

title: Plot: p50/p95/p99 latency histogram (3-stage)
description: THE headline plot. Three stages measured independently: (a) ITCH parse, (b) LOB book-update, (c) strategy-to-order roundtrip. p50/p95/p99 reported for each. These numbers go top-of-fold in the README (E4A). Methodology: warmup events excluded, hardware + build flags documented in REPRODUCIBILITY.md.
close_reason: Replay events now carry parse/book-update/strategy latency fields; report.py renders a latency histogram and p50/p95/p99 table for the three stages.

title: Validate failure-injection mock against real IBKR paper
description: Run both side-by-side for a session against IBKR paper. Any divergence in observed callback patterns is a mock bug. CRITICAL: without this, all subsequent failure-mode tests are unfounded.

title: Implement deterministic 1-minute CI slicer
description: Tool that takes a full-day ITCH file and produces a byte-identical 1-minute slice. Required for CI: full-day file is too large to download in CI runs. Slice generator must be deterministic across runs (same input -> same bytes out). Output committed to expected/ as the canonical CI test input.
close_reason: Added deterministic slicer with stdin support, generated local ignored 09:30-09:31 slice, and committed slice checksum/parameters

title: Schema versioning policy in docs/event-log-schema.md
description: Formal compat policy: schema bumps are breaking; both engines must update together; schema regression test in CI. Documents which fields are stable vs experimental.

title: Write docs/solutions/paper-soak-learnings.md
description: THE most valuable doc the project will ever produce. Every operational incident during the soak documented honestly: reconnects, rejects, missing data, slippage surprises. Includes a section on 'what's still papered-over' that we know about.

title: Doc audit (rustdoc examples on every pub item)
description: Every public API has at least one rustdoc example. cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --all-features builds zero warnings. Examples themselves are doctested.

title: bin/replay.ml (JSONL in, JSONL trades out)
description: Reads schema-versioned JSONL event log from stdin or file; emits JSONL trades to stdout. Same trade format as the Rust LOB so diff is byte-comparable. Errors (unhandled event, schema mismatch) go to stderr + non-zero exit.

title: v0.13 F6: TWS restart mid-position (reconnect drill)
description: Process survives TWS restart. Reconnect logic queries IBKR's open-positions, reconciles against audit log, resumes monitoring. Test target: reconcile within 30s of TWS coming back up.

title: v0.11 — Replay
description: First post-hardening release. Ship one reproducible NASDAQ TotalView-ITCH 5.0 case study with REPRODUCIBILITY.md. Move repo from 'library with claims' to 'library with evidence.' 2-3 weeks, no breaking changes. See plan_v0.11.md (local, uncommitted) for full DRAFT spec.

title: Apply version bumps for v0.11
description: nanobook 0.10.0 -> 0.11.0; nanobook-python 0.10.0 -> 0.11.0. Other crates untouched. Update Cargo.lock + python/pyproject.toml. No breaking changes.

title: Published wheel CI matrix (Linux + macOS)
description: GitHub Actions matrix builds maturin-based wheels for x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-apple-darwin, on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12. Uploads as release artifacts (not yet published to PyPI — that's a v0.13+ decision). Establishes the wheel-build pipeline competitors (Nautilus, Hummingbot, LEAN) all have as credibility signal.
